name: Physics

aliases.name: Body
aliases.comment:
A body is pure physics state — position, velocity, orientation.
All bodies start out centered on the origin; use [moveTo](#moveTo) to set the position.

Use [block](#block), [plane](#plane), [sphere](#sphere), or [cylinder](#cylinder)
for simple bodies, or [dynamic](#dynamic) and [static](#static) for bodies
made of multiple [shapes](Physics-Shape#Shape).

aliases.name: BodyCoordinates
aliases.comment:
Coordinate system of a body, used for defining
[shapes](Physics-Shape#Shape) and [constraints](Physics-Constraint#Constraint)
relative to the body’s origin.

aliases.name: Config
aliases.comment:
Configures a simulation.

    onEarth =
        { gravity = Vector3d.gees 0 0 -1
        , duration = Duration.seconds (1 / 60)
        , solverIterations = 20
        , contacts = emptyContacts
        , constrain = \_ -> Nothing
        , collide = \_ _ -> True
        }

  - `gravity` — set the gravity vector, or `Vector3d.zero` for no gravity

  - `duration` — set to `Duration.seconds (1 / 60)` for 60 fps

  - `solverIterations` — balance between precision and performance, 20 is a sweet spot

  - `contacts` — pass [Contacts](#Contacts) from the previous frame for warm starting, or leave as default for cold start

  - `constrain` — limit body movement relative to each other, see [Constraint](Physics-Constraint#Constraint)

  - `collide` — decide which bodies can collide with each other.
aliases.type: { gravity : Vector3d.Vector3d Acceleration.MetersPerSecondSquared Physics.WorldCoordinates, duration : Duration.Duration, solverIterations : Basics.Int, contacts : Physics.Contacts id, constrain : id -> Maybe.Maybe (id -> List.List Physics.Constraint.Constraint), collide : id -> id -> Basics.Bool }

aliases.name: Contacts
aliases.comment:
Contacts from the most recent simulation frame. Contains contact points
and solver state for warm starting.

aliases.name: WorldCoordinates
aliases.comment:
Coordinate system of the simulation, used for positions and velocities
of bodies.

values.name: angularAccelerationFromTorque
values.comment:
Compute angular acceleration from torque: α = I⁻¹τ

How fast will this torque make the body spin up?
values.type: Physics.Body -> Vector3d.Vector3d Torque.NewtonMeters Physics.WorldCoordinates -> Vector3d.Vector3d AngularAcceleration.RadiansPerSecondSquared Physics.WorldCoordinates

values.name: angularVelocity
values.comment: Get the current angular velocity of a body.

values.name: angularVelocityDeltaFromAngularImpulse
values.comment:
Compute angular velocity change from an angular impulse: Δω = I⁻¹L

How much does this impulse add to my current spin?
values.type: Physics.Body -> Vector3d.Vector3d (Quantity.Product Torque.NewtonMeters Duration.Seconds) Physics.WorldCoordinates -> Vector3d.Vector3d AngularSpeed.RadiansPerSecond Physics.WorldCoordinates

values.name: applyForce
values.comment:
Apply a force at a point on a body.

Keep applying the force every simulation step to accelerate.

    force =
        Vector3d.withLength (Force.newtons 50)
            Direction3d.positiveY

    pushedBox =
        box
            |> applyForce force pointOnBox
values.type: Vector3d.Vector3d Force.Newtons Physics.WorldCoordinates -> Point3d.Point3d Length.Meters Physics.WorldCoordinates -> Physics.Body -> Physics.Body

values.name: applyImpulse
values.comment:
Apply an impulse at a point on a body, adding to its velocity and angular velocity.

    impulse =
        Vector3d.withLength
            (Quantity.times (Duration.seconds 0.005)
                (Force.newtons 50)
            )
            Direction3d.positiveY

    hitCueBall =
        cueBall
            |> applyImpulse impulse hitPoint
values.type: Vector3d.Vector3d (Quantity.Product Force.Newtons Duration.Seconds) Physics.WorldCoordinates -> Point3d.Point3d Length.Meters Physics.WorldCoordinates -> Physics.Body -> Physics.Body

values.name: applyInverseInertia
values.comment:
Apply the inverse inertia tensor of a body to a vector.
Returns Vector3d.zero for static bodies.
For common cases, see [angularAccelerationFromTorque](#angularAccelerationFromTorque)
and [angularVelocityDeltaFromAngularImpulse](#angularVelocityDeltaFromAngularImpulse).
values.type: Physics.Body -> Vector3d.Vector3d units Physics.WorldCoordinates -> Vector3d.Vector3d (Quantity.Rate units (Quantity.Product Mass.Kilograms Area.SquareMeters)) Physics.WorldCoordinates

values.name: block
values.type: Block3d.Block3d Length.Meters Physics.BodyCoordinates -> Physics.Material.Material Physics.Material.Dense -> Physics.Body

values.name: centerOfMass
values.comment: Get the center of mass of a body. Returns Nothing for static bodies.

values.name: contactPoints
values.comment:
Get contact points from the most recent simulation frame, filtered by a predicate.
Each entry is a pair of body ids and a list of world-space contact points between them.

    crash =
        Physics.contactPoints
            (\a b -> a == "jeep" && b == "wall")
            contacts
values.type: (id -> id -> Basics.Bool) -> Physics.Contacts id -> List.List ( id, id, List.List (Point3d.Point3d Length.Meters Physics.WorldCoordinates) )

values.name: cylinder
values.comment:
Create a cylinder, approximated with 12 side faces.
For more subdivisions, use [dynamic](#dynamic) with [Shape.cylinder](Physics-Shape#cylinder).
values.type: Cylinder3d.Cylinder3d Length.Meters Physics.BodyCoordinates -> Physics.Material.Material Physics.Material.Dense -> Physics.Body

values.name: damp
values.comment:
Set linear and angular damping, in order to decrease velocity over time.
These parameters specify the proportion of velocity lost per second.
Inputs are clamped between 0 and 1, the defaults are 0.01.
values.type: { linear : Basics.Float, angular : Basics.Float } -> Physics.Body -> Physics.Body

values.name: dynamic
values.comment:
Create a dynamic body from shapes and materials. Mass and center of mass
are derived from geometry and density.

values.name: emptyContacts
values.comment: Empty contacts for the first simulation frame (no warm starting).

values.name: frame
values.comment:
Get the position and orientation of the body in the world as Frame3d.
Useful to transform points and directions between world and body coordinates,
e.g. for rendering.
values.type: Physics.Body -> Frame3d.Frame3d Length.Meters Physics.WorldCoordinates { defines : Physics.BodyCoordinates }

values.name: mass
values.comment: Get the mass of a body. Returns Nothing for static bodies.

values.name: moveTo
values.comment:
Set the position of the body in the world,
e.g. to raise a body 5 meters above the origin:

    movedBody =
        body
            |> moveTo (Point3d.meters 0 0 5)

values.name: onEarth
values.comment:
A ready-to-use simulation config with Earth gravity pointing down (-Z)
at 60 fps. Customize it by updating individual fields,
see [Config](#Config) for available options.

values.name: originPoint
values.comment: Get the origin point of a body in the world.

values.name: place
values.comment:
Set the position and orientation of a body. Like [moveTo](#moveTo)
but also sets the orientation.

    placedBody =
        body
            |> place
                (Frame3d.atPoint (Point3d.meters 2 0 1)
                    |> Frame3d.rotateAround Axis3d.z (Angle.degrees 45)
                )

Left-handed frames are not supported — Z is recomputed from X and Y.
values.type: Frame3d.Frame3d Length.Meters Physics.WorldCoordinates { defines : Physics.BodyCoordinates } -> Physics.Body -> Physics.Body

values.name: plane
values.comment:
Create a static plane, collidable only from the direction of the normal,
e.g. for +Z:

    floor =
        Physics.plane Plane3d.xy Material.wood
values.type: Plane3d.Plane3d Length.Meters Physics.BodyCoordinates -> Physics.Material.Material any -> Physics.Body

values.name: pointMass
values.comment: Create a point mass — infinitely small, so it doesn’t collide with other point masses.
values.type: Point3d.Point3d Length.Meters Physics.WorldCoordinates -> Mass.Mass -> Physics.Material.Material any -> Physics.Body

values.name: raycast
values.comment:
Find the closest intersection of a ray against a list of bodies.

  - point masses are always excluded because they are infinitely small
  - a plane only intersects when the ray is facing the plane’s normal
values.type: Axis3d.Axis3d Length.Meters Physics.WorldCoordinates -> List.List ( id, Physics.Body ) -> Maybe.Maybe ( id, Physics.Body, { point : Point3d.Point3d Length.Meters Physics.WorldCoordinates, normal : Direction3d.Direction3d Physics.WorldCoordinates } )

values.name: rotateAround
values.comment:
Rotate the body around an axis in the world,
e.g. to rotate a body 45 degrees around the Z axis:

    rotatedBody =
        body
            |> rotateAround Axis3d.z (Angle.degrees 45)
values.type: Axis3d.Axis3d Length.Meters Physics.WorldCoordinates -> Angle.Angle -> Physics.Body -> Physics.Body

values.name: scaleMassTo
values.comment:
Scale a body to the given mass. The volume and center of mass
are preserved. Has no effect on static bodies.

values.name: setAngularVelocityTo
values.comment:
Replace the angular velocity of a body. See [setVelocityTo](#setVelocityTo)
for guidance. Has no effect on static bodies.

values.name: setVelocityTo
values.comment:
Replace the linear velocity of a body.

For physics-correct changes, prefer [applyImpulse](#applyImpulse). Using
`setVelocityTo` after `applyImpulse` silently discards the impulse:

    body
        |> applyImpulse impulse point
        -- erased by the next line
        |> setVelocityTo newVelocity

Has no effect on static bodies.

values.name: simulate
values.comment:
Simulates one frame. Returns updated bodies and contacts.
Call this on a message from the `onAnimationFrame` subscription
with [onEarth](#onEarth) to simulate 1/60th of a second in Earth gravity:

    ( simulated, contacts ) =
        simulate onEarth model.bodies

To improve solver stability for stacked objects, pass contacts
from the previous frame back via the config’s `contacts` field:

    ( simulated, contacts ) =
        simulate { onEarth | contacts = model.contacts }
            model.bodies
values.type: Physics.Config id -> List.List ( id, Physics.Body ) -> ( List.List ( id, Physics.Body ), Physics.Contacts id )

values.name: sphere
values.type: Sphere3d.Sphere3d Length.Meters Physics.BodyCoordinates -> Physics.Material.Material Physics.Material.Dense -> Physics.Body

values.name: static
values.comment:
Create a static body from shapes and materials.
Static bodies only collide with dynamic bodies, not other static bodies.

values.name: translateBy
values.comment:
Move the body relative to its current position,
e.g. to translate a body down by 5 meters:

    translatedBody =
        body
            |> translateBy (Vector3d.meters 0 0 -5)

values.name: velocity
values.comment: Get the current linear velocity of a body.

values.name: velocityAt
values.comment:
Get the linear velocity of a point on a body.
Takes into account both linear and angular velocities.
values.type: Point3d.Point3d Length.Meters Physics.WorldCoordinates -> Physics.Body -> Vector3d.Vector3d Speed.MetersPerSecond Physics.WorldCoordinates

aliases.name: Constraint
aliases.comment:
Limit the freedom of movement of two bodies relative to each other.

Pass a `constrain` function in the [simulation config](Physics#Config). For example, to drag
a body with the mouse, connect a mouse to a point on the box with
[pointToPoint](#pointToPoint):

    constrain id =
        if id == "mouse" then
            Just
                (\otherId ->
                    if otherId == "box" then
                        [ pointToPoint Point3d.origin pointOnBox ]

                    else
                        []
                )

        else
            Nothing

values.name: distance
values.comment:
Keep the centers of mass of two bodies at the constant distance
from each other.

values.name: hinge
values.comment:
Keep two bodies connected with each other and limit the freedom of rotation.
Useful for e.g. connecting a window to a window frame, or to connect a wheel to a car.
values.type: Axis3d.Axis3d Length.Meters Internal.Coordinates.BodyCoordinates -> Axis3d.Axis3d Length.Meters Internal.Coordinates.BodyCoordinates -> Physics.Constraint.Constraint

values.name: lock
values.comment: Keep two bodies connected with each other and remove all degrees of freedom between bodies.
values.type: Frame3d.Frame3d Length.Meters Internal.Coordinates.BodyCoordinates {} -> Frame3d.Frame3d Length.Meters Internal.Coordinates.BodyCoordinates {} -> Physics.Constraint.Constraint

values.name: pointToPoint
values.comment:
Connect a point on the first body with a point on the second body.
This doesn’t limit the freedom of rotation of two bodies.
values.type: Point3d.Point3d Length.Meters Internal.Coordinates.BodyCoordinates -> Point3d.Point3d Length.Meters Internal.Coordinates.BodyCoordinates -> Physics.Constraint.Constraint

name: Physics.Material

unions.name: Dense
unions.comment:
Material with density, required for dynamic bodies
where mass is computed from geometry.

unions.name: Surface
unions.comment:
Material with surface properties only — friction and bounciness,
no density. Used for static bodies and point masses.

aliases.name: Material
aliases.comment:
Material encodes friction, bounciness, and optionally density.

The type parameter tracks capabilities:

  - `Material Dense` — carries density (required for volumetric bodies)
  - `Material Surface` — surface properties only (for static bodies and point masses)

**Friction** controls how much a body resists sliding against another.
0 means frictionless (like ice), 1 means maximum grip (like rubber).

**Bounciness** (coefficient of restitution) controls how much kinetic energy
is preserved after a collision. 0 means no bounce (the body absorbs the impact),
1 means a perfectly elastic bounce.

When two shapes collide, their friction values are combined using the geometric
mean √(f1 · f2), so a slippery surface dominates. Bounciness uses the
maximum of the two values, so the bouncier surface wins.

values.name: dense
values.comment:
Create a dense material.

Density is clamped to at least 1 kg/m³. Friction and bounciness are clamped to [0, 1].
values.type: { density : Density.Density, friction : Basics.Float, bounciness : Basics.Float } -> Physics.Material.Material Physics.Material.Dense

values.name: ice
values.comment: Density 900 kg/m³, friction 0.03, bounciness 0.1.

values.name: rubber
values.comment: Density 1100 kg/m³, friction 0.8, bounciness 0.7.

values.name: steel
values.comment: Density 7800 kg/m³, friction 0.3, bounciness 0.2.

values.name: surface
values.comment:
Create a surface material.

Friction and bounciness are clamped to [0, 1].
values.type: { friction : Basics.Float, bounciness : Basics.Float } -> Physics.Material.Material Physics.Material.Surface

values.name: wood
values.comment: Density 700 kg/m³, friction 0.4, bounciness 0.3.

name: Physics.Shape

aliases.name: Shape
aliases.comment:
Shapes are needed for creating compound [dynamic](Physics#dynamic)
and [static](Physics#static) bodies.

The supported primitive shapes are [block](#block), [sphere](#sphere),
and [cylinder](#cylinder). For complex geometry use [unsafeConvex](#unsafeConvex).

Shapes within a body **should not overlap** — composing shapes only affects physical
properties like mass, inertia, and center of mass. Use [plus](#plus) and
[minus](#minus) to combine shapes, for example to create hollow bodies.

values.name: cylinder
values.comment:
Create a cylinder shape with the given number of side faces, clamped to at least 3.
Even numbers are more efficient, because collision performance depends
on the number of unique non-parallel faces and edges.

values.name: minus
values.comment:
Subtract the first shape from the second. The subtracted shape must be
fully contained within the other. It reduces volume, mass, and inertia,
and is excluded from collision detection.

Useful for hollow bodies.

    crate =
        Shape.block outer
            |> Shape.minus (Shape.block inner)

values.name: plus
values.comment:
Add a shape to another.

    snowman =
        Shape.sphere bottom
            |> Shape.plus (Shape.sphere top)

values.name: sum
values.comment:
Combine a list of shapes.

    dumbbell =
        Shape.sum
            [ Shape.cylinder 12 leftWeight
            , Shape.cylinder 12 bar
            , Shape.cylinder 12 rightWeight
            ]

values.name: unsafeConvex
